script:
Up.
Feet on the floor.
Shorts on.
Drink water now.

David Goggins ran 100 miles on two broken feet.
He was up at 4am logging miles before the city moved.
Not to feel ready.
Because the work was there and he showed up.

You swore last night:
do the morning lift before opening any work doc.
That is still true.
The alarm just fired.
Nothing has changed.

no one is coming, it's on you.

Not your boss.
Not your past version.
Not the version of you who checks Slack first.
This version.
Right now.
Shorts already on.

Walk to the gym.
Front door open, feet moving, that is the whole job.
One lift.
Before one doc.
The bar is loaded.
Get under it.

  "script": "Up.

Shorts. Now.

Goggins ran 100 miles on two stress-fractured feet.
He didn't negotiate with the bed.
He put his body in motion and let the mind catch up.

You swore last night:
do the morning lift before opening any work doc.

no one is coming, it's on you.

So here is what happens next.
Shorts on.
Door open.
Feet moving toward the gym.
Not toward the phone.
Not toward the inbox.
The gym.

One door between you and the bar.
Open it.",
